title: Lab 41: IPv6 Networking
description: Configure persistent IPv6 networking and hostname resolution on the client system

tasks:
Configure the active clientvm connection with the following persistent IPv6 settings:

IPv6 Address: fd00:122:41::25/64
IPv6 Gateway: fd00:122:41::1
DNS Server: fd00:122:41::53

Set the following persistent identity and resolution settings on clientvm:

Hostname: clientvm.ipv6lab.local
Static host entry: servervm.ipv6lab.local
Address: fd00:122:41::3
Leave the existing IPv4 configuration unchanged.
hints: Use nmcli so the change survives a reconnect.
checks: CONN="$(nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: '$2 == "eth1" {print $1; found = 1; exit} $2 != "" && $2 != "lo" && first == "" {first = $1} END {if (!found) print first}')"; test -n "$CONN"; test "$(nmcli -g ipv6.addresses connection show "$CONN" | tr -d '\\')" = 'fd00:122:41::25/64' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv6.gateway connection show "$CONN" | tr -d '\\')" = 'fd00:122:41::1' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv6.dns connection show "$CONN" | tr -d '\\')" = 'fd00:122:41::53' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv6.method connection show "$CONN")" = 'manual'
solution_commands: CONN="$(nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: '$2 == "eth1" {print $1; found = 1; exit} $2 != "" && $2 != "lo" && first == "" {first = $1} END {if (!found) print first}')"
nmcli connection modify "$CONN" ipv6.method manual ipv6.addresses fd00:122:41::25/64 ipv6.gateway fd00:122:41::1 ipv6.dns fd00:122:41::53 connection.autoconnect yes
CONN="$(nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: '$2 == "eth1" {print $1; found = 1; exit} $2 != "" && $2 != "lo" && first == "" {first = $1} END {if (!found) print first}')"
